novel_features.name: SLA Breach Countdown
novel_features.description: Shows every open ticket projected to breach SLA within the next N hours, sorted by minutes remaining — act before the clock runs out, not after.
novel_features.rationale: Requires computing due_by minus now across the entire open-ticket corpus and joining to group/agent tables; paginating the API in real time burns rate-limit budget and takes too long.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an SRE or IT admin needs to know which tickets will breach SLA before the next check-in — prevents reactive firefighting.

novel_features.name: My Queue
novel_features.description: Combines all tickets assigned to you with SLA countdown plus any change records awaiting your approval — the first command an agent runs each morning.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-entity join across open tickets and pending change approvals for a single agent identity in one offline query; the API requires three separate calls with manual correlation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to get an AI agent's complete pending workload in one structured call before deciding which task to action next.

novel_features.name: Cross-Entity Full-Text Search
novel_features.description: Runs a single ranked full-text search across tickets, assets, change records, and KB articles simultaneously — find everything related to an incident keyword in one shot.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite FTS5 virtual tables across all synced entity corpora with unified relevance ranking; the Freshservice API has per-entity search endpoints with no cross-entity ranking.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an AI agent needs to gather context about a symptom across all ITSM entities before proposing a resolution.

novel_features.name: Agent Workload Heatmap
novel_features.description: Table of agents with open ticket count, average ticket age, P1/P2 count, and normalized load score — see who is drowning and who has capacity in five seconds.
novel_features.rationale: Groups the full open-ticket corpus by assignee and joins to group membership; the Freshservice UI shows per-agent queues only one agent at a time with no comparative load score.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an AI agent needs to decide which human agent to assign a new ticket to based on current capacity.

novel_features.name: Change Collision Detector
novel_features.description: Flags change records whose planned maintenance windows overlap, optionally filtered by CI — prevents two teams from scheduling conflicting downtime on the same system.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a self-join on the changes table over time ranges optionally crossed with affected CI lists; no Freshservice UI view surfaces overlapping windows across different groups.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before approving a change to verify no other group has a conflicting maintenance window on the same infrastructure.

novel_features.name: Incident Recurrence Fingerprinter
novel_features.description: Uses FTS similarity on ticket subjects and descriptions to surface repeated symptom patterns grouped by asset, requester, or keyword — shows which problems keep coming back.
novel_features.rationale: Requires FTS across the full historical ticket corpus plus asset and requester joins; API approach exhausts rate limits after a few hundred tickets and provides no text-similarity ranking.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to identify root-cause candidates when an AI agent is investigating a chronic incident pattern.

novel_features.name: Knowledge Gap Finder
novel_features.description: Matches recent ticket subjects against the KB article corpus using FTS and ranks topic clusters with no matching article by ticket volume — tells you exactly what to document first.
novel_features.rationale: Requires simultaneous FTS across two corpora (tickets and KB articles) with gap-detection logic; the Freshservice portal has no view correlating ticket volume to KB coverage.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an AI agent is drafting a knowledge base improvement plan and needs to prioritize which gaps to fill.

novel_features.name: Asset Orphan Detector
novel_features.description: Finds assets with no associated open ticket, no active contract, and no assigned user activity in the last N days — surfaces hardware you are paying maintenance on that nobody uses.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a left-join across assets, tickets, contracts, and users in SQLite; no Freshservice UI combines all four entity types into a single orphan view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this during IT asset audits to identify candidates for decommission or reallocation without manual cross-referencing.

novel_features.name: Department SLA Leaderboard
novel_features.description: Aggregates SLA compliance percentage, breach count, and mean time to resolve by requester department for a rolling period — exec-ready ranking without exporting to Excel.
novel_features.rationale: Requires grouping the full resolved-ticket history by department field across a multi-week window; the Freshservice Analytics module is a separate paid add-on.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an AI agent is generating an executive SLA compliance report or identifying departments that need service level attention.

novel_features.name: On-Call Coverage Gap Finder
novel_features.description: Identifies time windows where high-severity tickets arrived but no agent in the group acknowledged within SLA — surfaces staffing gaps in on-call rotations.
novel_features.rationale: Requires correlating ticket created_at and first-response timestamps against group membership across a multi-week history; no Freshservice endpoint provides this temporal view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to identify on-call schedule gaps before the next incident strikes the same window.

narrative.headline: Every Freshservice operation in one Go binary — with offline search, SLA intelligence, and agent-native JSON that no other Freshservice tool has.
narrative.value_prop: freshservice-pp-cli is the first general-purpose terminal CLI for Freshservice — the jira-cli equivalent for ITSM. It covers tickets, changes, assets, users, and knowledge base with full CRUD, local SQLite sync, FTS across all entity types, and novel analytics commands that would require three dashboards and a spreadsheet to replicate manually.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authenticate with a Personal Access Token (PAT) from your Freshservice account. Set FRESHSERVICE_APIKEY=<your-pat> and FRESHSERVICE_DOMAIN=<yourcompany.freshservice.com>, then run `freshservice-pp-cli doctor` to verify connectivity.

quickstart.comment: verify your PAT and domain are configured correctly
pull all tickets, assets, changes, and users into the local store
see your assigned tickets and pending approvals in one view
which tickets will breach SLA in the next 4 hours
find related tickets and KB articles before creating a duplicate
see which agents are overloaded before assigning a ticket

troubleshoots.fix: verify FRESHSERVICE_APIKEY is your PAT (not username/password) and FRESHSERVICE_DOMAIN is yourcompany.freshservice.com without https://
wrap filter query in quotes: --query '"status:2 AND priority:3"' — Freshservice requires double-quoted filter strings
your plan rate limit is being hit; reduce --per-page or add --delay between bulk operations
check FRESHSERVICE_DOMAIN matches your actual subdomain; run doctor --verbose for connection details

narrative.when_to_use: Use freshservice-pp-cli when an AI agent needs to query, create, or update ITSM records in Freshservice — ticket triage, change approval workflows, asset inventory checks, or SLA compliance reporting. It is especially powerful for cross-entity correlation tasks (finding all open tickets linked to an asset, checking change window collisions, or surfacing knowledge gaps) that would require multiple API calls and manual data joining if done via raw HTTP.

recipes.title: Triage the morning ticket queue
recipes.explanation: pull high-priority open tickets via Freshservice's filter syntax with only the fields needed for triage routing

recipes.title: Check SLA risk before standup
recipes.explanation: surface every ticket that will breach its SLA in the next 4 hours so the team can act proactively

recipes.title: Find recurring issues on a server
recipes.explanation: fingerprint repeated incident patterns linked to a specific asset over 90 days

recipes.title: Check for change window conflicts
recipes.explanation: detect overlapping maintenance windows before approving a new change

recipes.title: Get full context before creating a ticket
recipes.explanation: cross-entity search to find existing tickets, related assets, and KB articles before opening a duplicate

trigger_phrases: who has the most open tickets
